name: Yuzu-Cured Kampachi with Frozen Olive Oil, Compressed Melon & Smoked Dashi Air
description: A Michelin-level crudo featuring lightly yuzu-cured kampachi, vacuum-compressed melon, frozen olive oil shards, and smoked dashi air. A balance of temperature, aroma, acidity, and texture using modernist techniques.
keywords: kampachi crudo, yuzu cure, molecular gastronomy, fine dining seafood, compressed fruit, smoked dashi air, modernist cuisine

steps.instruction: Prepare the cure: Dissolve salt and sugar into yuzu and lime juice. Add kombu and let infuse for 20 minutes.
Cure the kampachi: Slice into thick sashimi pieces. Submerge in curing liquid for exactly 12 minutes. Remove, rinse lightly, pat dry, and chill.
Compress the melon: Combine melon, cucumber juice, rice vinegar, sugar, and salt. Vacuum seal at full compression and release slowly until melon becomes translucent.
Prepare frozen olive oil: Drizzle olive oil onto an anti-griddle or freeze in a thin layer. Scrape into delicate shards just before serving.
Make citrus gel: Heat yuzu juice and sugar, whisk in agar, and boil for 1 minute. Set into a thin sheet, then blend into a smooth gel and transfer to a piping bottle.
Create smoked dashi air: Warm dashi slightly and add soy lecithin. Use an immersion blender or ultrasonic homogenizer to form a stable foam. Infuse with cherry wood smoke under a cloche.
Plate: Arrange kampachi slices in an overlapping arc. Add compressed melon cubes. Pipe citrus gel dots precisely. Scatter finger lime pearls and micro shiso.
Finish: Add frozen olive oil shards last. Season lightly with flaky salt and a few drops of white soy.
Serve: Present under a smoked cloche and release tableside. Spoon smoked dashi air onto the dish just before serving.

winePairings.name: Junmai Daiginjo Sake
winePairings.notes: Elegant and aromatic, enhancing the delicate citrus and umami without overpowering the fish.

winePairings.name: Champagne Blanc de Blancs
winePairings.notes: High acidity and fine bubbles cut through the richness while elevating the citrus notes.
